task: If I'm 30, plan to retire at 65, and can save $300/month, with a 3% annual return, 13% current tax rate, and 24% retirement tax rate, show the comparison chart between Traditional and Roth IRA.
graderReasoning: The agent navigated to Chase’s IRA calculator page, entered the requested inputs (age 30, retirement age 65, monthly contribution $300, 3% return, 13% current tax rate, 24% retirement tax rate), and opened the calculator’s chart table. The table on the Chase page showed the resulting comparison values at retirement year 2061: Deductible Traditional IRA $197,000, Non-deductible Traditional IRA $201,000, and Roth IRA $224,000. The agent then returned to the chart view and provided a screenshot artifact of the visible comparison chart. This satisfies the request to show the comparison chart between Traditional and Roth IRA with the specified assumptions.
final_result_response:
Done. I entered:

- Age: 30
- Retire at: 65
- Monthly savings: $300
- Annual return: 3%
- Current tax rate: 13%
- Retirement tax rate: 24%
- Starting balance: $0

Chase’s IRA calculator shows the estimated 2061 values as:

| Account type | Estimated value |
|---|---:|
| Roth IRA | $224k |
| Non-deductible Traditional IRA | $201k |
| Deductible Traditional IRA | $197k |

![Chase IRA comparison chart](./artifacts/chase_ira_comparison.png)

The Roth IRA is highest in this scenario.
action_history: Opening Chase: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.chase.com/');
Returning to chart view: await page.locator('f2e10').click();
